summary: Strengthened the executable workflow-worker specification and startup readiness evidence. The broader resolver binding, portable-core, rollout, capability-failure, canary, and documentation work remains for later bounded remediation attempts.

gapStatuses.evidence: WorkflowWorkerSpec now owns workflow classes/types, task queues, activity identities, and a composition fingerprint; it validates loaded Temporal type names against declarations before Worker construction. The complete multi-fleet activity-handler composition is not yet centralized in this type.
No changes in this attempt; provenance, digest, policy, compatibility, and portable fallback require a cohesive skill-resolution contract change.
No changes in this attempt; shared provider-neutral core and cross-host fixtures remain absent.

gapStatuses.requirement: Readiness, build identity, and versioned deployment
gapStatuses.evidence: Readiness is now asserted only after Worker.run tasks are scheduled, and exposes build, image, deployment, resolver-core, queues, workflows, and fingerprint identities. Temporal deployment versioning, immutable identity enforcement, and drain/recreate controls remain.
No changes in this attempt; capability-failure classification, budget protection, diagnostics, metrics, and alerts remain.

Added unit coverage proving queue/activity changes alter the executable fingerprint and readiness exposes deployment identities. Parent-child, replay/restart, parity, and Compose canary coverage remain.
No documentation changes were made in this attempt.

targetedLocalChecks.details: Repository preliminary audits passed, but /usr/local/bin/python reported: No module named pytest.

knownRemainingGaps: Centralize complete multi-fleet activity composition in the executable spec.
Implement trusted native binding eligibility independently of publish authority.
Extract a shared portable resolver core and parity fixtures.
Add immutable/versioned Temporal deployment and stale-worker controls.
Add deployment capability failure classification and observability.
Add parent-child, replay/restart, parity, and Compose canary tests.
Update the MM-1202 architecture, operations, restart, and failure documentation.
scopeNotes: Changes are limited to gaps explicitly listed in the latest verification report.
No pull request was created and Jira status was not changed.
